transcripts.title: Stop Listening to Trump: Larry Fink Controls When Iran War Ends
transcripts.transcript:
Stop listening to Trump. If you want to know when the Iran war is going to end, there is only one person you need to listen to, not Trump. Stop listening to Trump. The only person you need to pay attention to is **Larry Fink**. He runs **BlackRock**, the biggest wealth management firm in the world. He controls most of the world's money.

Listen to what he said about the Iran war last week. You're about to find out when the war will end. "If there's a cessation of war and yet Iran remains a threat, a threat to trade, a threat to the **Straits of Hormuz**, then I would argue that we could have years, years of, you know, above $100, closer to $150 oil. What happens to the global economy if that happens? We'll have global recession."

You just heard the man that controls the most amount of money in the world say the Iran war cannot end with Iran controlling the Strait of Hormuz. The United States needs to take control of the Strait of Hormuz or there will be a global recession.

So when I keep saying on this page, this Iran war is being fought to **tokenize everything** and create a new global financial system, guess where I got it from? "We believe the next step going forward will be the **tokenization of financial assets**." And when I tell you that Iran represents a leakage in this new global financial tokenized system, guess where I got it from? "$4.1 trillion of money in sitting globally in digital wallets. A lot of that money's outside the United States. If we could tokenize."

You're listening to these bullshit headlines then ruining relationships with your family and friends. Don't do that. Listen to me. Then go ruin the relationships with your family and friends, duh.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🚨 STOP SCROLLING! Hindi si Trump ang may control kung kailan matatapos ang Iran war. ALAM MO BA KUNG SINO? 👀

It's Larry Fink. CEO ng BlackRock. Yung pinakamalaking wealth management firm sa WORLD. Siya ang may hawak ng pera ng buong planeta. 💰

Dito ka magpakatanda: Last week, sinabi niya clearly - kung mananatiling threat ang Iran sa Strait of Hormuz, oil prices aakyat to $150. Global recession? GARANTISADO.

So ang real talk: The Iran war isn't about politics. It's about CONTROL. Control ng trade routes. Control ng money flow.

And here's the plot twist 🎯 - This war is being fought to TOKENIZE EVERYTHING. Yeah, yung $4.1 TRILLION na naka-digital wallets globally? They want to tokenize all of that.

Iran = leakage sa bagong global financial system. Period.

So next time ka mag-scroll ng headlines, tanungin mo sarili mo: Are you getting the real story? Or just noise?

Drop a comment if you want more deep dives like this. 👇 #BlackRock #Iran #Tokenization #Finance

transcripts.title: The #1 Exercise for Growing New Brain Cells
transcripts.transcript:
What's the number one exercise for growing new brain cells? Nope, it's not a crossword puzzle, it's not supplements, it's not brain games, it's not meditation, though all of these can probably help. The answer probably will surprise you.

I'm a board-certified emergency medicine physician, and this will definitely keep you out of my ER. I'm talking about **resistance training**.

Your brain doesn't stop growing because you age. It stops growing because it stops being challenged. And here's the science. Lifting weights stimulates the growth of new brain cells, especially in the **hippocampus**, the part of your brain that's responsible for memory, learning, and focus.

When you lift weights, your body releases **BDNF**. Think of BDNF like **fertilizer for your brain**. It helps grow new brain cells and improves memory, attention, and your learning speed. And this can happen at any age. Young adults, midlife, even older adults who have never lifted before.

Resistance training doesn't just make your muscles stronger, it makes communication between brain cells faster and more resilient. That's why people who lift regularly show better brain function, slower brain aging, and lower dementia risk.

So if you want a stronger brain, not just a stronger body, pick up the weights. Your brain will thank you.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🧠 ANONG PINAKAMABILIS NA PARAAN para tumubo ng NEW BRAIN CELLS?

Hint: Hindi crossword puzzles. Hindi supplements. Hindi brain games.

The answer will SHOCK you. 😱

Ito ay RESISTANCE TRAINING. Oo, yung pagbuhat ng weights! 💪

Science time: When you lift, your body releases BDNF - parang FERTILIZER para sa brain mo. Tumutubo ng NEW brain cells sa hippocampus (yung part responsible sa memory, learning, focus).

Best part? Works at ANY AGE! Young adult ka man, midlife, or senior na never nag-lift - pwede pa ring mag-grow ng brain cells.

Your brain doesn't stop growing kasi tumatanda ka. It stops growing kasi HUMAHANTO NA SIYA SA CHALLENGE.

So kung gusto mo ng:
✅ Better memory
✅ Faster learning
✅ Lower dementia risk

Pick up those weights! Your brain will thank you. 🙏

Tag someone who needs to hear this! 👇 #BrainHealth #ResistanceTraining #BDNF #Fitness

transcripts.title: The Highest Paying Job Now: Social Media Customer Acquisition
transcripts.transcript:
I think the job that gets you the most money now is if you have talents in social media for customer acquisition. I used to pay those guys 48 grand a year, now they're 250,000. Because you can measure their work based on customer acquisition every week.

So most of them become contractors, you know, they make half a million dollars a year because they know how to take content, turn it into a 59 second ad on social and acquire 200 customers. That's a very specific.

So remember, everybody say you have to be an engineer, an engineer, an engineer. Now you want to be an artist, again, write stories, acquire customers, know how the social media platforms work, know how to post on TikTok versus LinkedIn versus Twitter versus Instagram.

Those people in their early 20s are so valuable now. So if you know how to use your phone, somebody wants to hire you. So if you were to go back in time with the specific skill set that you currently possess, is that what you would do?

I would use my editing skills to make 15s, 30s and 59s. And I would basically say, look, pay me, you know, 100 bucks for each customer I acquire for you. I'd make millions.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
💰 PINAKAMATAAS NA SAHOD NGAYON? HINDI ENGINEER.

It's SOCIAL MEDIA CUSTOMER ACQUISITION. Let me explain.

Used to pay these guys $48K/year. NOW? $250K+. Some contractors making $500K+! 🤯

Why? Kasi MEASURABLE yung work nila. Every week, makikita mo exactly how many customers they acquired.

The skill? Take content → turn it into 59-second ads → acquire 200 customers. That's IT.

Everyone's like "be an engineer, be an engineer." Pero ang truth? You want to be an ARTIST who knows:
✅ How to write stories
✅ How to acquire customers
✅ TikTok vs LinkedIn vs Twitter vs Instagram

Young people in their 20s who know how to use their phone? SO VALUABLE RIGHT NOW.

Performance-based pricing is the game changer: "Pay me $100 per customer acquired."

Do the math: 200 customers = $20K. Multiply that. You'll make MILLIONS. 💸

Save this if you're choosing a career path! #CareerAdvice #SocialMedia #CustomerAcquisition #Money

transcripts.title: The AI Job Market Split: Commodity vs Irreplaceable Work
transcripts.transcript:
I'm not going to sugarcoat this one. There are people in creative work who are losing their jobs right now, copywriters, translators, and illustrators, for example. And some of this is short-sighted on the part of the companies doing it, but some of it isn't.

There's an economics concept that explains what's happening. When the cost of producing something drops to nearly zero, demand doesn't go to infinity, it saturates. There are only so many blog posts you can actually read, only so many product descriptions, only so many social media graphics. At some point, more supply just doesn't matter because nobody wants any more.

So think about those ads in your feed, like, for example, Oregon drivers delighted by this new insurance change. You know the ones, they used to be stock photography. Someone took those pictures and someone got paid for that. But mostly now they're AI generated. And guess what? Mostly no one cares. No one ever really cared. The image was kind of a filler anyway. It just existed in that, filling in that rectangle. And filling a rectangle is not a job that survives when a machine can fill it for free.

That's the painful truth. AI didn't devalue that work. It revealed what the market actually valued it at. So some work that felt like creative work was really a commodity all along and AI just made it visible.

But it's also more complicated because saturation and an explosion are happening at the same time. So yes, the commodity content saturates. That part is real and it's very painful. But vibe coding, for example, is simultaneously blowing open a universe of problems that software never reached before. Problems that were too local, too specific, too contextual for any commercial product to justify building.

So economists call this **Jevons paradox**. And when a resource gets dramatically cheaper, consumption doesn't top out. It expands into the territory that was always there but never reachable.

So two things are true at the same time here. There's a simple way to look at it. If your work is in the rectangle, commodity, replicable, indistinguishable from what a machine produces, that market's collapsing and it isn't coming back. But if your work is 20 years of frontline knowledge about a specific hard problem that no one ever built software for, that market is just opening.

So we're getting this market split. Commodity goes to zero, specific, contextual, irreplaceable goes up and the middle disappears.

So is your work closer to filling the rectangle or is it the thing that no amount of free output can actually replace?

I'm Helen from the Artificiality Institute. Follow for more on this series and how to think about AI in your job. I've studied this for a decade and I really wanna get some more nuance into this discussion.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
⚠️ HARSH TRUTH: May mga creative workers na NAWAWALAN NG JOBS right now.

Copywriters. Translators. Illustrators. Real talk - AI is replacing them.

Pero bakit? Here's the economics lesson: When cost drops to ZERO, demand doesn't go to infinity. It SATURATES. 📉

Think about those stock photos in ads? "Oregon drivers delighted by insurance change." 😐 Someone got paid for that before. Now? AI generates it for FREE.

Nobody cared naman. Filler lang siya. "Filling the rectangle."

Here's the painful truth: AI didn't devalue that work. It REVEALED what the market actually valued it at. ZERO.

BUT WAIT - may plot twist! 🎯

Jevons Paradox: When something gets cheaper, consumption EXPANDS into new territory.

So while commodity work is dying, may NEW markets na bumubukas. Problems that were TOO SPECIFIC, TOO CONTEXTUAL for software before - NOW reachable.

The market is SPLITTING in two:
❌ Commodity work → Goes to ZERO
✅ Specific, contextual, irreplaceable → Goes UP
📉 The MIDDLE → DISAPPEARS

Tanong: Is your work "filling the rectangle"? Or is it the thing that NO AMOUNT OF FREE AI OUTPUT can replace?

20 years of frontline knowledge > Generic content. Always.

Follow for more AI truth bombs. 💣 #AI #Jobs #CareerAdvice #JevonsParadox

transcripts.title: Bezos wants to be right again
transcripts.transcript:
Jeff Bezos just raised **$100 billion** to prove that every major AI company is focused on the wrong thing. And many of the smartest people in the world agree with him.

For three years, every AI story has been about screens, writing, coding, designing. The entire revolution has been digital. But Jeff Bezos doesn't care about your laptop.

The Wall Street Journal and New York Times both reported this week that the Amazon founder is raising **$100 billion** to buy manufacturing companies, chip makers, defense contractors, aerospace firms, and automate them with physics simulating AI.

Investor documents call it a "manufacturing transformation vehicle" - same size as Softbank's Vision Fund, the largest tech investment vehicle ever, except Softbank bet on apps and Bezos is betting on factories.

The brain is Project Prometheus, which is co-founded with Vic Bajaj, a physicist who built Google X, the lab behind Waymo and Wing. Some decent brains there.

Prometheus is building AI that doesn't understand language, it understands physics: how air flows around a wing, where metal cracks under pressure, how to simulate an entire production line before a single machine turns on.

Bezos is co-CEO for the first time since Amazon, getting back in the game, hiring from OpenAI and DeepMind. So the people who built the language models are now building the physics models.

And he is not alone in this thesis. Two weeks ago, Yann LeCun, the Turing Award winner who literally invented neural networks behind modern AI, left Meta after 12 years and raised just over **$1.1 billion** to build world models. **$1.03 billion** just to flex on us.

So AI trained on cameras and sensors instead of text, AI that predicts the next state of a physical system rather than the next sentence that you have in mind. Backed by NVIDIA, Bezos Expedition, Samsung, Toyota.

Two weeks before that, Stanford's Fei-Fei Li, who basically launched the modern deep learning revolution, raised a billion dollars for the same idea.

Two Turing class researchers and the richest man alive are all making the same bet. That many billions means it might be time to pay attention.

The **$100 billion** fund buys the companies, Prometheus installs the brain: one acquires, the other transforms. This is a full conveyor belt of futuristic transformation.

Bezos is meeting sovereign wealth funds in Abu Dhabi and Singapore, the same ones that are watching their energy infrastructure get destroyed right now. Qatar just lost **17%** of its LNG capacity for five years.

These funds are all looking for a post-oil industrial thesis and Bezos handily walked in with one.

If you look at the other end of the same economy, DoorDash is now paying gig workers a few dollars to film themselves washing dishes so that robots can learn how and take their place.

So the full vision is that Bezos is buying the factory, DoorDash is paying the workers three dollars to train the machine that replaces the next worker. Similar thesis, different tax brackets.

So the physical AI era is coming. If Bezos is as right as he seems to think he is and as early, he's gonna have a lot more money for bottle service.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🚨 BEZOS JUST RAISED $100 BILLION. And everyone's asking: WHY?

For 3 years, AI has been all about DIGITAL stuff. Writing. Coding. Designing. Screens lang.

Pero si Bezos? Hindi siya interesado sa laptop mo. 🏭

He's raising $100 BILLION to buy:
✅ Manufacturing companies
✅ Chip makers
✅ Defense contractors
✅ Aerospace firms

Then automate them all with PHYSICS-SIMULATING AI.

Project Prometheus - yung "brain" - is building AI that understands PHYSICS, not language. How air flows around wings. Where metal cracks. How to simulate entire factories BEFORE building them.

And he's NOT ALONE:
🧠 Yann LeCun (Turing Award winner) - Raised $1.1B for world models
🧠 Fei-Fei Li (Stanford legend) - Raised $1B for the same thing

These are the people who BUILT modern AI. And they're all making the SAME BET.

Physical AI > Digital AI

The $100B fund BUYS the companies. Prometheus INSTALLS the brain. Full conveyor belt of transformation.

Plot twist: While Bezos is buying factories, DoorDash is paying workers $3 to film themselves washing dishes... para turuan yung robots na papalit sa kanila. 🤖

Same thesis. Different tax brackets. 💸

The PHYSICAL AI ERA is coming. Are you ready?

Follow for more tech deep dives! #Bezos #AI #PhysicalAI #TechNews

transcripts.title: Manipulación: Evita las Trampas Comunes
transcripts.transcript:
The first is never explain yourself under pressure. So that automatically means submission and you might want to fire back with, you know, I don't think you meant to but that question is designed to put me in a position of justifying myself and that's not a role I'm going to take right now.

Number two is never argue about your motives. So just don't do it. Just openly call it out like, well, the way you said that puts me into a place of defending what kind of person I am.

Three is never take the bait on any character question, any character questions at all. Never argue about who you are.

Four is never apologize just to deescalate ever. So if you're in with a manipulator, the apology that they're trying to force out of you is an admission of guilt, not saying that you're going to fix anything.

And five is never match emotion to prove your point. So never match somebody's intensity.

Six is never accept somebody else's language without precision. So words like disrespect or abuse or betrayal, they can totally hijack a narrative. So you might just say, you know, those words all carry some kind of frame and if I accept those words, what you just said, I'm not speaking for myself anymore. So that's a big one.

And seven is never accept any binary traps like the either yes, no, the either or question. There's always nuance to it and always seek specificity, always get into specifics. When you said this, what specifically did you want to talk about? And always go back to their intention, like, what was your intention? What's the ideal outcome here for you?

And eight is never try to win through just logic.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🛡️ 8 WAYS TO SHUT DOWN A MANIPULATOR.

Save this. You'll need it.

1️⃣ NEVER explain yourself under pressure. Auto-move na 'to sa defense. Instead say: "That question is designed to make me justify myself. Not doing that."

2️⃣ NEVER argue about your motives. Call it out: "You're putting me in a position to defend who I am. Nope."

3️⃣ NEVER take the bait on character questions. Don't argue about who you are.

4️⃣ NEVER apologize just to deescalate. Sa manipulator, apology = admission of guilt. Hindi para mag-fix.

5️⃣ NEVER match their emotion. Intensity nila? Don't mirror it.

6️⃣ NEVER accept their language. Words like "disrespect," "abuse," "betrayal" - mga frame hijackers. Say: "If I accept those words, I'm not speaking for myself anymore."

7️⃣ NEVER accept binary traps. Yes/No questions? May nuance always. Ask: "What specifically did you mean? What's your ideal outcome?"

8️⃣ NEVER try to win through logic alone.

Boundaries = self-respect. 🙌

Share this with someone who needs it! #Boundaries #Manipulation #MentalHealth #SelfRespect

transcripts.title: A billionaires advice
transcripts.transcript:
Do me a favor, the next board meeting, shut your fucking mouth. Don't say a fucking word. Every time you open your mouth, you put your foot in it.

My first business was the meatball shop, and I opened up this restaurant in New York City in 2010, and it took off like wildfire, dude. Eventually, a big investor came in, the guy who founded Ruby Tuesdays, this guy, Sandy Beal. And after the board meeting, Sandy said, hey, Mike, can I take you out for a cup of coffee? I was like, yeah, of course. And I'm thinking he's gonna sit me down and be like, I'm so proud of you, you're killing it.

And he sits down at the coffee table with me in this little coffee shop, and he goes, can I be frank with you? Learn to listen. You don't grow from hearing your own voice. You grow from listening. Shut your mouth. You talk too much. I was like, white in the face, mortified. You don't need to say everything you think. You don't need to react on everything you hear. Pause, pause. The power's in the pause.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🤫 THE BEST BUSINESS ADVICE I EVER GOT?

"Shut your mouth. You talk too much."

Story time: First business ko - meatball shop sa NYC. 2010. Took off like WILDFIRE. 🔥

Big investor came in. Sandy Beal (founder ng Ruby Tuesdays). After board meeting, kumausap siya: "Coffee tayo?"

Akala ko? Gonna praise me. "Proud of you, you're killing it!"

Pero dito ka magpakatanda:

"Learn to listen. You don't grow from hearing your own voice. You grow from LISTENING."

I was WHITE IN THE FACE. Mortified. 😅

But he's RIGHT. You don't need to say everything you think. You don't need to react to everything you hear.

PAUSE. The power's in the PAUSE.

Next meeting mo? Try it. Listen more. Talk less. Watch what happens.

Agree? Drop a 🔥 #BusinessAdvice #Leadership #Listening #Entrepreneur

transcripts.title: The AI Job Market Split in Two. One Side Pays $400K and Can't Hire Fast Enough.
transcripts.transcript:
So there are essentially infinite AI jobs right now, not growing demand, not a hot sector, none of that is true, it is functionally infinite. And that's true for businesses employing 10 to 20 people as much as it is true for businesses employing hundreds of 1000s. There is no functional upper limit to what employers would love to have as far as AI talent, and they cannot find them.

After hundreds of interviews on particular roles, I'm hearing from employers, we can't fill the role. And I hear you saying, Nate, that must be a lie. I have applied for hundreds of AI positions. I am good at AI, it is not working. I get it. We have a K shaped job market right now. And there is a split in what employers want that is confusing this issue.

**The AI labor market is actually two markets moving in opposite directions:**

**Market 1:** Traditional knowledge work roles - generalist, product managers, standard software engineers, conventional business analysts. Job opening count is flat or falling.

**Market 2:** Roles that design, build, operate and manage AI systems. Growing fast. The ratio of AI jobs to AI talent right now is 3.2 to 1 - three plus AI jobs for every single qualified candidate.

**7 Critical AI Skills Employers Are Desperate For:**

1. **Specification Quality** - Writing clear intent for agents
2. **Evals** - Testing and measuring AI output quality
3. **Multi-Agent Task Decomposition** - Breaking work into manageable segments for agents
4. **Failure Pattern Recognition** - Diagnosing agent failures (context degradation, specification drift, sycophantic confirmation, tool selection errors, cascading failures, silent failures)
5. **Trust and Security Design** - Building guardrails, understanding cost of error, reversibility, verifiability
6. **Context Architecture** - Building the "Dewey Decimal system for agents" - organizing company data so agents can find and use it correctly
7. **Cost and Token Economics** - Calculating ROI, choosing the right model for the task, understanding token costs

These skills are tied to how AI actually works. They're not going anywhere. Companies are betting careers on them and they're desperate for people who have them.
transcripts.tiktokScript:
🚨 INFINITE AI JOBS RIGHT NOW. But here's the catch...

Employers CAN'T HIRE FAST ENOUGH. Ratio is 3.2 AI jobs per 1 qualified candidate. 🤯

Pero bakit may mga hindi nakakakuha ng work? Kasi K-SHAPED MARKET tayo ngayon.

📉 Market 1 (DYING): Traditional knowledge work - generalists, product managers, standard engineers. Job openings: FLAT or FALLING.

📈 Market 2 (EXPLODING): AI system builders. Growing FAST.

7 SKILLS NA HINAHANAP NG EMPLOYERS:

1️⃣ Specification Quality - Writing clear intent for AI agents
2️⃣ Evals - Testing AI output quality
3️⃣ Multi-Agent Task Decomposition - Breaking work for agents
4️⃣ Failure Pattern Recognition - Diagnosing why agents fail
5️⃣ Trust & Security Design - Building guardrails
6️⃣ Context Architecture - Organizing data for agents
7️⃣ Cost & Token Economics - ROI, model selection, token costs

These skills AREN'T GOING ANYWHERE. Companies are BETTING CAREERS on them.

Tanong: Nasaan ka? Market 1 or Market 2? 🤔

Save this if you're upskilling! #AI #Jobs #CareerAdvice #TechSkills

transcripts.title: IT'S OVER: Trump and Iran Just Shut Out the UK and NATO
transcripts.transcript:
Yesterday, President Trump made clear that the discussions with Iran are real, and asked why he trusted the process he dropped this bombshell: "Because they're going to make a deal. They did something yesterday that was amazing, actually. They gave us a present, and the present arrived today. It was a very big present, worth a tremendous amount of money."

**Who's at the Table (The New Diplomatic Architecture):**
- Egypt, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan gathered in Riyadh
- Egyptian intelligence opened direct channel to Iran's Revolutionary Guard
- Turkey worked phones between Iran and Egypt
- Oman carried messages on maritime security
- Pakistan offered to host comprehensive settlement talks
- All members of Trump's "Board of Peace" - architecture from Abraham Accords

**Who's NOT at the Table:**
- No British Foreign Secretary
- No European Commission representative  
- No NATO quartet
- UK, EU, NATO completely absent from Middle East diplomacy

**Key Developments:**
- Lebanese government banned Hezbollah, told it to turn over weapons
- Palestinian Authority condemned Iran, sided with Saudi Arabia
- Hamas considering disarmament proposal from Trump's Board of Peace
- Iran's "axis of resistance" has crumbled - military capacity shattered, supply lines severed

**Europe's Collapse:**
- EU fast-tracked $750B LNG deal with US after months of delays
- Brussels pulled scheduled vote to ban Russian oil imports - "can no longer afford the ideology"
- Kirill Dmitriev (Russia's Sovereign Wealth Fund): "Green agenda, Russophobia, no oil, no gas, no seat at the table"
- Europe facing fuel shortage within days (Shell boss warning)

**Ukraine's Predicament:**
- Zelensky on "Don't Forget About Me Tour" - met King Charles 5x, addressed British parliament 2x
- But Britain has no energy cards left to play
- "The war in Iran is not a distraction from Ukraine - it's a clarifying lens of new strategic geometry"
- Europe can't fund it, Britain can't underwrite it, US has moved on to bigger stage

**Conclusion:** "This is a new world, being shaped by the principles of economic and political sovereignty and led by Donald Trump."
